title: Blood Pressure Screening in Adults
recommendation: Screen for high blood pressure in adults 18 years or older. Obtain measurements outside of the clinical setting for diagnostic confirmation before starting treatment.

title: Colorectal Cancer Screening
recommendation: Screen for colorectal cancer in all adults aged 45 to 75 years.

title: Breast Cancer Screening
recommendation: Screen for breast cancer with mammography every 2 years for women aged 40 to 74 years.

title: Cervical Cancer Screening
recommendation: Screen for cervical cancer in women aged 21 to 65 years with cytology (Pap smear) every 3 years or, for women aged 30 to 65 years, every 5 years with high-risk HPV testing.

title: Prediabetes and Type 2 Diabetes Screening
population: Adults 35–70 who are overweight or obese
recommendation: Screen for prediabetes and type 2 diabetes in adults aged 35 to 70 years who have overweight or obesity.

title: Lipid Disorders Screening in Younger Adults
recommendation: Screen for lipid disorders in adults at increased risk of cardiovascular disease.

title: Lung Cancer Screening
recommendation: Screen for lung cancer with annual low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) in adults aged 50 to 80 years who have a 20 pack-year smoking history and currently smoke or have quit within the past 15 years.

title: Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Screening
recommendation: Screen for abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA) with a one-time abdominal duplex ultrasonography in men aged 65 to 75 years who have ever smoked.

title: Osteoporosis Screening
population: Women 65+ or postmenopausal women <65 at elevated risk
recommendation: Screen for osteoporosis with bone measurement testing (DEXA scan) to prevent osteoporotic fractures in women 65 years and older.

title: Depression Screening in Adults
recommendation: Screen for depression in the general adult population, including pregnant and postpartum women. Screening should be implemented with adequate systems in place to ensure accurate diagnosis, effective treatment, and appropriate follow-up.

title: Thyroid Dysfunction Screening
recommendation: Current evidence is insufficient to assess the balance of benefits and harms of screening for thyroid dysfunction in non-pregnant, asymptomatic adults.
